items.title: Show HN: Vibe-coding games with Claude, day 93: Rock Paper Scissors Lizard Spock
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer has been vibe-coding and shipping a new HTML5 game every single day for 93 days straight using Claude. It is a masterclass in maintaining momentum and using AI direction to get real projects out the door instead of getting stuck in planning loops. This week, look at their daily release history to see how they scope down their daily goals, and commit to shipping just one small, visible UI polish to your AI-ingest app today.

items.title: Show HN: An open-source Claude skill that stops AI building the wrong app
items.why_it_matters: A veteran product manager built an open-source Claude skill designed to stop AI from running off and building the wrong features during vibe-coding sessions. It directly helps you improve your "direction" game by forcing a quick alignment check before the AI writes code. This week, copy their prompt framework into your Claude Code system instructions to keep your next feature build tightly scoped and on track.

items.title: Shiploop – an autonomous delivery loop for Claude Code, pitch to MVP
items.why_it_matters: This tool creates an autonomous delivery loop specifically for Claude Code, helping you move from a raw pitch to an MVP. Since you are already using Claude Code to build AI-ingest, this can help automate the repetitive parts of setting up new features. This week, clone the repo and run it against a single feature idea for AI-ingest to see if it can scaffold the Next.js page for you.

items.title: Show HN: Alluvia – mine your Claude Code/Cursor/ChatGPT history, locally
items.why_it_matters: Alluvia lets you locally search and mine your Claude Code, Cursor, and ChatGPT history. Since you are building your second project, being able to quickly find the exact prompts and solutions you used on your first Next.js/Supabase project is incredibly valuable. This week, install Alluvia locally and search your history for "Supabase auth" or "Vercel deploy" to instantly reuse your past working setups.

items.title: Oya – Keep tool outputs away from the LLM to cut tokens and stop injection
items.why_it_matters: Oya is a tool that filters and keeps raw tool outputs away from the LLM, which helps cut down on token usage and prevents prompt injection. Since you previously rated token-saving strategies highly, this is a practical way to keep your API costs down as you scale your AI-ingest tool. This week, look at their GitHub integration examples and try wrapping one of your Next.js API routes to see if it lowers your Claude API usage.
